reasoning: The input contains multiple functions (including .init and a main-like function in .text) that were analyzed independently as required.
Basic blocks were identified based on control-flow instructions (jmp, je, jne, call, ret) and branch targets, following the definition of a maximal linear sequence of instructions.
Intraprocedural control flow was mapped by analyzing branch targets and fall-through behavior within each function.
Call instructions and interprocedural transitions (e.g., PLT stubs, libc functions) were treated as part of the basic block, not as separate blocks or CFG edges.
Uncertain or complex indirect control flow (like those involving GOT/PLT lookups) was omitted from the edges, while the rest of the intraprocedural CFG was preserved.
